subject: Economics
chapter: Forms of Market and Price Determination

concepts.title: Perfect Competition
concepts.description: Perfect competition is a market structure where a large number of firms produce a homogeneous product. It is characterized by free entry and exit, perfect knowledge, and no barriers to entry. It leads to a perfectly competitive market equilibrium, where the supply and demand curves intersect.

concepts.title: Monopoly
concepts.description: A monopoly is a market structure where a single firm supplies the entire market with a product. It is characterized by barriers to entry, which prevent other firms from entering the market. The monopolist has complete control over the market and can set the price and quantity of the product.

concepts.title: Monopolistic Competition
concepts.description: In a monopolistically competitive market, a large number of firms produce differentiated products. There are barriers to entry, but they are not as high as in a monopoly. Firms can set their own prices, but they are influenced by the prices of their competitors.

concepts.title: Perfect Competition vs Monopoly
concepts.description: Students often confuse perfect competition with monopoly. They think that perfect competition has only one firm, but it actually has many firms. The key difference is that in perfect competition, firms are price-takers, while in a monopoly, the firm is a price-maker.

concepts.title: Price Determination in Different Market Structures
concepts.description: Students should remember that in a perfectly competitive market, price is determined by the intersection of the supply and demand curves. In a monopoly, the price is determined by the monopolist's marginal revenue and marginal cost. In a monopolistic competition, the price is determined by the intersection of the firm's supply curve and the demand curve for its product.

concepts.title: OLIGOPOLY
concepts.description: One Large Inevitable Group Of Powerful Organizations. This mnemonic helps students remember the characteristics of an oligopoly market structure.

concepts.title: Monopsony
concepts.description: A monopsony is a market structure where a single buyer purchases the entire market supply of a product. It is characterized by barriers to entry, which prevent other buyers from entering the market. The monopsonist has complete control over the market and can set the wage and quantity of the product.
